h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a smooth trajectory of correctly identifying force labels and was expected to continue doing so.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Earth pulls cart. [moves to the up arrow] Track pushes cart. [caps the pen, slides the finished sheet aside, reaches for the next]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new labelling task in writing, completing the required device, but this consolidates an already-established correct answer rather than enacting a genuinely new criterion or representation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The diagram rotation in T2 introduces a mild spatial perturbation and a compact reorienting phrase, but there is no stock-taking contrast showing the old route has failed, and the tutor continues the same naming task rather than introducing a changed device or evidence standard.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The phrase gestures at a transferability principle but does not give the learner an actionable new criterion or representation distinct from the original naming task, making it a renamed continuation of the same route rather than a fitted new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can occur; T3 simply confirms and closes the task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The phrase gestures at a transferability principle but does not give the learner an actionable new criterion or representation distinct from the original naming task, making it a renamed continuation of the same route rather than a fitted new mechanism.
